title: Train and tram drivers
description: Operate underground loading or moving machine to load or move coal, ore, or rock using shuttle or mine car or conveyors. Equipment may include power shovels, hoisting engines equipped with cable-drawn scraper or scoop, or machines equipped with gathering arms and conveyor. Directly supervise and coordinate activities of material-moving machine and vehicle operators and helpers. Supervise and coordinate activities of passenger attendants. (Based on 11 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Inspect vehicles and check gas, oil, and water levels prior to departure.
Operate locomotives to transport freight or passengers between stations or to assemble or disassemble trains within rail yards.
Report delays, accidents, or other traffic and transportation situations, using telephones or mobile two-way radios.
Prepare reports regarding any problems encountered, such as accidents, signaling problems, unscheduled stops, or delays.
Inspect locomotives after runs to detect damaged or defective equipment.
Monitor gauges or meters that measure speed, amperage, battery charge, or air pressure in brake lines or in main reservoirs.
Inspect couplings, air hoses, journal boxes, and handbrakes to ensure they are securely fastened and functioning properly.
Operate track switches, derails, automatic switches, and retarders to change routing of train or cars.
Receive, relay, and act upon instructions and inquiries from train operations and customer service center personnel.
Monitor loading processes to ensure that materials are loaded according to specifications.
Drive machines into piles of material blasted from working faces.
Confer with conductors or traffic control center personnel via radiophones to issue or receive information concerning stops, delays, or oncoming trains.
Signal other workers to set brakes and to throw track switches when switching cars from trains to way stations.
Greet passengers, provide information, and answer questions concerning fares, schedules, transfers, and routings.
Observe and respond to wayside and cab signals, including color light signals, position signals, torpedoes, flags, and hot box detectors.
Drive locomotives to and from various stations in roundhouses to have locomotives cleaned, serviced, repaired, or supplied.
Check to ensure that brake examination tests are conducted at shunting stations.
Inspect the condition of stationary trains, rolling stock, and equipment.
Inspect tracks for defects such as broken rails and switch malfunctions.
Test vehicle equipment, such as lights, brakes, horns, or windshield wipers, to ensure proper operation.
Complete accident reports when necessary.
Check the condition of a vehicle's tires, brakes, lights, fuel, and safety equipment to ensure everything is in working order.
Collect fares or vouchers from passengers, and make change or issue receipts as necessary.
Drive vehicles or operate machines or equipment to transport passengers or complete work assignments.
Communicate with dispatchers by radio, telephone, or computer to exchange information and receive service requests.
Inspect or test vehicles and equipment to ensure that they are safe and free of defects.
Maintain or verify records of time, materials, or crew activities.
Prepare, compile, and submit reports on work activities or work-related incidents.
Plan work assignments and equipment allocations to meet transportation or production goals.
Enforce safety rules and regulations among workers and operations.
Confer with customers, supervisors, or contractors to exchange information or resolve problems.
Resolve worker problems or collaborate with employees to assist in problem resolution.
Train workers in proper operational procedures and company policies.
Plan and establish schedules for operations or personnel.
Requisition necessary personnel, supplies, equipment, or repair services.
